trigger.from_breadth: 4662 with the Replicating-Directory-Changes GUID seen (Surface-2 W4 mandatory), OR run unconditionally each pass

telemetry_required: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4662 + winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security + event_data with the replication GUID + SubjectUserName (CONFIRMED flowing all 4 GLs)
telemetry_gap_behavior: 4662 is collected on all 4 GLs so this module is fully live. The downstream actor-sourcing is partially blind: 4768/4769 are DROPPED at collection, so DO NOT expect Kerberos to source the actor - pivot to 4624 Type 3/10 + 4776 NTLM + FortiGate instead, and note the Kerberos gap.

method.compute: capture SubjectUserName, winlogbeat_host_name, timestamp for each hit

method.goal: Classify the requesting account (this is the whole detection)
method.compute: is SubjectUserName a Domain Controller machine account (ends with $ and in the DC allow-list) OR the Entra/AAD Connect sync account (MSOL_* / AAD_*)?
method.decision_input: DC machine account or known sync account = EXPECTED replication (benign). ANY other account requesting replication-changes = DCSync

method.goal: Source the actor behind an anomalous account
method.compute: recent 4624 logon source IP + LogonType (3/10), 4776 NTLM validation host; correlate to an RDP session or external IP
method.note: Kerberos 4768/4769 are dropped - cannot use TGS/TGT; this NTLM+logon pivot is the available path

method.compute: off-hours (23:00-05:00)? first-ever replication request for this account?

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: replication-directory-changes requested by a NON-DC, NON-sync account = DCSync credential theft
decision.benign: DC machine account or known Entra Connect sync account = normal AD replication (no finding)

false_positive_checks: DC machine accounts (maintain a DC allow-list per environment)
Entra/Azure AD Connect sync account (MSOL_* / AAD_*) - has legitimate replication rights
